zh.lifeSummary: 當Giorgio II 'Nano' del Vasto Marquis of Ceva於1210年出生在義大利皮蒙特辛薩盧左時，他的父親Guglielmo II de Ceva, Marquis de Ceva31歲，母親Marie del Vasto De Saluzzo20歲。他去世於1268年5月24日在義大利享年58歲，埋葬在義大利皮蒙特辛塞瓦。

nameMeanings.name: del
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French (Lorraine): variant of the habitational name Delle 2 or a masculine form of a homonymous topographic name. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Spanish or Italian preposition with fused definite article del ‘of the’, which forms first part of surnames such as Del Cid and Del Signore , has apparently been in some instances counted as an independent surname.

variants.name: Delle
meanings: German: habitational name from a place so named in North Rhine-Westphalia or a topographic name (see   Dell  ).

French (Lorraine): habitational name from Delle, a place in Territoire de Belfort. Alternatively, a topographic name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, for someone who lived near a prominent alder tree or trees, from elle, a derivative of Middle High German erle ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: Vasto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Reynaldo, Alejandro, Andres, Carlito, Humberto, Jaime, Javier, Lilia, Luiz, Luz.  Portuguese Guilherme.
Portuguese and Galician: habitational name from any of the places called Basto in Portugal, from basto ‘dense (wood)’. This surname is also found in southwestern Spain.

nameMeanings.name: Giorgio
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: 'Nano'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Muslim name, from Persian: ‘lady, princess, bride’.

en.lifeSummary: When Giorgio II 'Nano' del Vasto Marquis of Ceva was born in 1210, in Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy, his father, Guglielmo II de Ceva, Marquis de Ceva, was 31 and his mother, Marie del Vasto De Saluzzo, was 20. He died on 24 May 1268, in Italy, at the age of 58, and was buried in Ceva, Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Giorgio II 'Nano' del Vasto Marquis of Ceva est né en 1210, à Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italie, son père, Guglielmo II de Ceva, Marquis de Ceva, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Marie del Vasto De Saluzzo, avait 20 ans. Il est décédé le 24 mai 1268, à Italie, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Ceva, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Giorgio II 'Nano' del Vasto Marquis of Ceva 1210 in Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guglielmo II de Ceva, Marquis de Ceva 31 und seine Mutter Marie del Vasto De Saluzzo 20. Er starb am 24. Mai 1268 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Italien und wurde in Ceva, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Giorgio II 'Nano' del Vasto Marquis of Ceva è nato il nel 1210 nel Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italia, il suo padre, Guglielmo II de Ceva, Marquis de Ceva, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Marie del Vasto De Saluzzo, aveva 20 anni. Morì il 24 maggio 1268, nel Italia, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Ceva, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italia.

ja.lifeSummary: Giorgio II 'Nano' del Vasto Marquis of Cevaが1210年にイタリアピエモントクウネオサルズゾで生まれた時，父Guglielmo II de Ceva, Marquis de Cevaは31歳，母Marie del Vasto De Saluzzoは20歳であった。彼は1268年5月24日にイタリアで58歳で死去し，イタリアピエモントクウネオセヴァにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Giorgio II 'Nano' del Vasto Marquis of Ceva(이)가 1210년 이탈리아 피몽 쿤오 살루즈오에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guglielmo II de Ceva, Marquis de Ceva(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Marie del Vasto De Saluzzo(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1268년 5월 24일 이탈리아에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 이탈리아 피몽 쿤오 세바에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Giorgio II 'Nano' del Vasto Marquis of Ceva nasceu em 1210, em Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Itália, seu pai, Guglielmo II de Ceva, Marquis de Ceva, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Marie del Vasto De Saluzzo, tinha 20 anos. Ele faleceu em 24 de maio de 1268, em Itália, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Ceva, Cuneo, Piemonte, Itália.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Giorgio II 'Nano' del Vasto Marquis of Ceva родился в 1210 г. в местности салуззо, кунео, пиемонт, Италия, его отцу по имени Guglielmo II de Ceva, Marquis de Ceva было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Marie del Vasto De Saluzzo, 20. Он умер 24 мая 1268 г. в местности Италия в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности кева, кунео, пиемонт, Италия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Giorgio II 'Nano' del Vasto Marquis of Ceva nació en 1210, en Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italia, su papá, Guglielmo II de Ceva, Marquis de Ceva, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Marie del Vasto De Saluzzo, tenía 20 años. Murió el 24 de mayo de 1268, en Italia, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Ceva, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italia.
